headline: POCA Allows 2-Year Renewable Detention; Section 15B Bars Merits Review by Court
context: The Prevention of Crime Act 1959, amended in 2014, lets an appointed Prevention Board impose 2-year detention orders renewable indefinitely. Section 15B bars judicial review of the Board's discretionary decisions except on procedural-compliance grounds.

cards.big: POCA detention orders run 2 years and renew indefinitely. Section 15B insulates the appointed Board's discretionary decisions from court review on merits.
cards.sub: Detainees enter via 60-day police investigation periods (24h initial + 21-day + 38-day extensions) before the Prevention Board hearing.

cards.big: Section 15B bars review of Board discretion. Courts retain jurisdiction over procedural-compliance grounds; the merits are substantially insulated.
cards.sub: Section 7B: Board members are appointed by the Yang di-Pertuan Agong. The Act provides for Inquiry Officer reports and Board decisions.

cards.big: Article 5 protects personal liberty. POCA operates as a special law under Article 149.
cards.sub: Article 149 validates laws designed to stop or prevent specified threats notwithstanding inconsistency with Articles 5, 9, 10, 13 and 79.

cards.big: SUHAKAM and Bar Council have recorded complaints from detainees in lower-income and marginalised communities.
cards.sub: Disaggregated official outcome data is not published. Without socioeconomic denominators, 'disproportionate' cannot be quantified; the reporting gap itself is structural.

cards.big: POCA is not an Article 150 emergency ordinance. It is an operationally active preventive-detention regime sitting outside a courtroom.
Like a registered-person watchlist decided by an appointed committee, with detention as the operating consequence.
cards.sub: Preventive detention allows confinement without a criminal trial. Article 151 provides limited procedural safeguards via an advisory board.

cards.big: SUHAKAM and the Bar Council have urged restoring judicial review of Board decisions and publishing disaggregated outcome data. Both proposals remain unenacted.
